go:426523.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:426523.question:
​​​​​If ' $\rightarrow$ ' denotes increasing order of intensity, then the meaning of the words [talk $\rightarrow$ shout $\rightarrow$ scream] is analogous to [please $\rightarrow$ __________ $\rightarrow$ pander].

Which one of the given options is appropriate to fill the blank?

  1. flatter
  2. flutter
  3. fritter
  4. frizzle

go:426524.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 2
go:426524.question:
​​​​​$\text{P}$ and $\text{Q}$ have been allotted a hostel room with two beds, a study table, and an almirah. $\mathrm{P}$ is an avid bird-watcher and wants to sit at the table and watch birds outside the window. $\text{Q}$ does not mind that as long as his bed is close to the ceiling fan.

Which one of the following arrangements suits them the most?

go:426521.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:426521.question:
​​​​The decimal number system uses the characters $0,1,2, \ldots, 8,9$, and the octal number system uses the characters $0,1,2, \ldots, 6,7$.

For example, the decimal number $12\left(=1 \times 10^{1}+2 \times 10^{0}\right)$ is expressed as $14\left(=1 \times 8^{1}+4 \times 8^{0}\right)$ in the octal number system.

The decimal number $108$ in the octal number system is

  1. $168$
  2. $108$
  3. $150$
  4. $154$

go:426520.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:426520.question:
​​​​A shopkeeper buys shirts from a producer and sells them at $20 \%$ profit. A customer has to pay ₹ $3,186.00$ including $18 \%$ taxes, per shirt. At what price did the shopkeeper buy each shirt from the producer?

  1. ₹ $2,500.00$
  2. ₹ $1,975.40$
  3. ₹ $2,250.00$
  4. ₹ $2,548.80$

go:426518.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:426518.question:
​​If, for non-zero real variables $x, y$, and real parameter $a>1$,
\[
x: y=(a+1):(a-1) \text {, }
\]
then, the ratio $\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right):\left(x^{2}+y^{2}\right)$ is

  1. $2 a:\left(a^{2}+1\right)$
  2. $a:\left(a^{2}+1\right)$
  3. $2 a:\left(a^{2}-1\right)$
  4. $a:\left(a^{2}-1\right)$

go:426522.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 6
go:426522.question:
​​​​In the given text, the blanks are numbered $\text{(i)-(iv)}$. Select the best match for all the blanks.

Following a row  $\text{(i)}$   the shopkeeper  $\text{(ii)}$_the price of a frying pan, the cook stood  $\text{(iii)}$  a row to withdraw cash $\text{(iv)}$  the ATM booth.

  1. $\text{(i)}$ with $\text{(ii)}$ over $\text{(iii)}$ at $\text{(iv)}$ with
  2. $\text{(i)}$ at $\text{(ii)}$ over $\text{(iii)}$ over $\text{(iv)}$ in
  3. $\text{(i)}$ with $\text{(ii)}$ over $\text{(iii)}$ in $\text{(iv)}$ at
  4. $\text{(i)}$ over $\text{(ii)}$ with $\text{(iii)}$ over $\text{(iv)}$ at

go:426516.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:426516.question:
​The chart below shows the data of the number of cars bought by Millennials and Gen $\text{X}$ people in a country from the year $2010$ to $2020$ as well as the yearly fuel consumption of the country (in Million liters).



Considering the data presented in the chart, which one of the following options is true?

  1. The percentage increase in fuel consumption from $2010$ to $2015$ is more than the percentage increase in fuel consumption from $2015$ to $2020$.
  2. The increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from $2015$ to $2020$ is less than the decrease in the number of Gen $\text{X}$ car buyers from $2010$ to $2015$.
  3. The increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from $2010$ to $2015$ is more than the decrease in the number of Gen $\text{X}$ car buyers from $2010$ to $2015$.
  4. The decrease in the number of Gen $\text{X}$ car buyers from $2015$ to $2020$ is more than the increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from $2010$ to $2015$.

go:426525.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 9
go:426525.question:
​​​​The assembly shown below has three teethed circular objects (Pinions) and two teethed flat objects (Racks), which are perfectly mating with each other. Pinions can only rotate clockwise or anti-clockwise staying at its own center. Racks can translate towards the left $(\leftarrow)$ or the right $(\rightarrow)$ direction.



If the object $\text{A}$ (Rack) is translating towards the right $(\rightarrow)$ direction, the correct statement among the following is

  1. Object $\text{B}$ translates towards the right direction.
  2. Object $\text{B}$ translates towards the left direction.
  3. Object $\mathrm{R}$ rotates in the anticlockwise direction.
  4. Object $\mathrm{Q}$ rotates in the clockwise direction.

go:426514.title: GATE 2024 Electrical | General Aptitude Question: 10
go:426514.question:
​​​​A surveyor has to measure the horizontal distance from her position to a distant reference point $\text{C}$. Using her position as the center, a $200 \mathrm{~m}$ horizontal line segment is drawn with the two endpoints $\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$. Points $\text{A}$, $\text{B}$, and $\text{C}$ are not collinear. Each of the angles $\angle \mathrm{CAB}$ and $\angle \mathrm{CBA}$ are measured as $87.8^{\circ}$. The distance (in $\mathrm{m}$ ) of the reference point $\mathrm{C}$ from her position is nearest to

  1. $2603$
  2. $2606$
  3. $2306$
  4. $2063$

go:422971.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:422971.question:
​​​​​If ' $\rightarrow$ ' denotes increasing order of intensity, then the meaning of the words [sick $\rightarrow$ infirm $\rightarrow$ moribund] is analogous to [silly $\rightarrow \_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \longrightarrow$ daft].

Which one of the given options is appropriate to fill the blank?

  1. frown
  2. fawn
  3. vein
  4. vain

go:422970.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 2
go:422970.question:
​​​​​​The $15$ parts of the given figure are to be painted such that no two adjacent parts with shared boundaries (excluding corners) have the same color. The minimum number of colors required is

  1. $4$
  2. $3$
  3. $5$
  4. $6$

go:422969.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:422969.question:
How many $4$-digit positive integers divisible by $3$ can be formed using only the digits $\{1,3,4,6,7\}$, such that no digit appears more than once in a number?

  1. $24$
  2. $48$
  3. $72$
  4. $12$

go:422968.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:422968.question:
​​​​​​The sum of the following infinite series is

\[
2+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{8}+\frac{1}{9}+\frac{1}{16}+\frac{1}{27}+\cdots
\]

  1. $11 / 3$
  2. $7 / 2$
  3. $13 / 4$
  4. $9 / 2$

go:422967.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:422967.question:
​​​​​​In an election, the share of valid votes received by the four candidates $\text{A, B, C}$, and $\mathrm{D}$ is represented by the pie chart shown. The total number of votes cast in the election were $1,15,000$, out of which $5,000$ were invalid.



Based on the data provided, the total number of valid votes received by the candidates $\mathrm{B}$ and $\mathrm{C}$ is

  1. $45,000$
  2. $49,500$
  3. $51,750$
  4. $54,000$

go:422966.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 6
go:422966.question:
​​​​​​Thousands of years ago, some people began dairy farming. This coincided with a number of mutations in a particular gene that resulted in these people developing the ability to digest dairy milk.

Based on the given passage, which of the following can be inferred?

  1. All human beings can digest dairy milk.
  2. No human being can digest dairy milk.
  3. Digestion of dairy milk is essential for human beings.
  4. In human beings, digestion of dairy milk resulted from a mutated gene.

go:422965.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 7
go:422965.question:
The probability of a boy or a girl being born is $1 / 2$. For a family having only three children, what is the probability of having two girls and one boy?

  1. $3 / 8$
  2. $1 / 8$
  3. $1 / 4$
  4. $1 / 2$

go:422964.title: GATE DS&AI 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:422964.question:
​​​​​Person $1$ and Person $2$ invest in three mutual funds $\text{A, B}$, and $\text{C}$. The amounts they invest in each of these mutual funds are given in the table.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline & \text{Mutual fund A} & \text{Mutual fund B} & \text{Mutual fund C} \\
\hline \text{Person} 1 & 10,000 & 20,000 & 20,000 \\
\hline \text{Person} 2 & 20,000 & 15,000 & 15,000 \\
\hline
\end{array}$$
At the end of one year, the total amount that Person $1$ gets is ₹$500$ more than Person $2$. The annual rate of return for the mutual funds $\text{B}$ and $\mathrm{C}$ is $15 \%$ each. What is the annual rate of return for the mutual fund $\mathrm{A}$?

  1. $7.5 \%$
  2. $10 \%$
  3. $15 \%$
  4. $20 \%$

go:426530.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:426530.question:
​​​If ' $\rightarrow$ ' denotes increasing order of intensity, then the meaning of the words

[ charm $\rightarrow$ enamor $\rightarrow$ bewitch] is analogous to [bored $\rightarrow \_\_\_\_\_\_ \longrightarrow$ weary]

Which one of the given options is appropriate to fill the blank?

  1. jaded
  2. baffled
  3. dead
  4. worsted

go:426528.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 2
go:426528.question:
​​​​$\text{P, Q, R, S}$, and $\text{T}$ have launched a new startup. Two of them are siblings. The office of the startup has just three rooms. All of them agree that the siblings should not share the same room.

If $\text{S}$ and $\text{Q}$ are single children, and the room allocations shown below are acceptable to all.
$$\begin{array}{|l|l|l|}
\hline \text{PR} & \text{TS} & \text{Q} \\
\hline
\end{array}$$$$\begin{array}{|l|l|l|}
\hline \text{PQ} & \text{RT} & \text{S} \\
\hline
\end{array}$$

then, which one of the given options is the siblings?

  1. $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{T}$
  2. $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{S}$
  3. $\mathrm{T}$ and $\mathrm{Q}$
  4. $\mathrm{T}$ and $\mathrm{R}$

go:426537.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:426537.question:
​​​​Five years ago, the ratio of Aman's age to his father's age was $1: 4$, and five years from now, the ratio will be $2:5$. What was his father's age when Aman was born?

  1. $28$ years
  2. $30$ years
  3. $35$ years
  4. $32$ years

go:426536.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:426536.question:
​​​​For a real number $x>1$,
$$\frac{1}{\log _{2} x}+\frac{1}{\log _{3} x}+\frac{1}{\log _{4} x}=1$$ The value of $x$ is

  1. $4$
  2. $12$
  3. $24$
  4. $36$

go:426533.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:426533.question:
The greatest prime factor of $\left(3^{199}-3^{196}\right)$ is

  1. $13$
  2. $17$
  3. $3$
  4. $11$

go:426532.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 7
go:426532.question:
​​​​​Four identical cylindrical chalk-sticks, each of radius $r=0.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ and length $l=10 \mathrm{~cm}$, are bound tightly together using a duct tape as shown in the following figure.



The width of the duct tape is equal to the length of the chalk-stick. The area (in $\mathrm{cm}^{2}$ ) of the duct tape required to wrap the bundle of chalk-sticks once, is

  1. $20(4+\pi)$
  2. $20(8+\pi)$
  3. $10(8+\pi)$
  4. $10(4+\pi)$

go:426531.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:426531.question:
The bar chart shows the data for the percentage of population falling into different categories based on Body Mass Index $\text{(BMI)}$ in $2003$ and $2023$.

 



Based on the data provided, which one of the following options is INCORRECT?

  1. The ratio of the percentage of population falling into overweight category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has increased in $20$ years.
  2. The ratio of the percentage of population falling into underweight category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in $20$ years.
  3. The ratio of the percentage of population falling into obese category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in $20$ years.
  4. The percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in $20$ years.

go:426526.title: GATE ECE 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 10
go:426526.question:
​​​Two identical sheets $\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$, of dimensions $24 \mathrm{~cm} \times 16 \mathrm{~cm}$, can be folded into half using two distinct operations, $\text{FO1}$ or $\text{FO2}$.

In $\text{FO1}$, the axis of folding remains parallel to the initial long edge, and in $\text{FO2}$, the axis of folding remains parallel to the initial short edge.

If sheet $\text{A}$ is folded twice using $\text{FO1}$, and sheet $\text{B}$ is folded twice using $\text{FO2}$, the ratio of the perimeters of the final shapes of $\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$ is

  1. $14:11$
  2. $11: 14$
  3. $18:11$
  4. $11: 18$

go:426508.title: GATE Mechanical 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 1
go:426508.question:
​​If ' $\rightarrow$ ' denotes increasing order of intensity, then the meaning of the words [smile $\rightarrow$ giggle $\rightarrow$ laugh] is analogous to [disapprove $\rightarrow$ _________$\rightarrow$ chide]

Which one of the given options is appropriate to fill the blank?

  1. reprove
  2. praise
  3. reprise
  4. grieve

go:426513.title: GATE Mechanical 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 3
go:426513.question:
​​​​​In the following series, identify the number that needs to be changed to form the Fibonacci series.


$1,1,2,3,6,8,13,21, \ldots$

  1. $8$
  2. $21$
  3. $6$
  4. $13$

go:426512.title: GATE Mechanical 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 4
go:426512.question:
​​The real variables $x, y, z$, and the real constants $p, q, r$ satisfy

\[
\frac{x}{p q-r^{2}}=\frac{y}{q r-p^{2}}=\frac{z}{r p-q^{2}}
\]

Given that the denominators are non-zero, the value of $p x+q y+r z$ is

  1. $0$
  2. $1$
  3. $p q r$
  4. $p^{2}+q^{2}+r^{2}$

go:426511.title: GATE Mechanical 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 5
go:426511.question:
Take two long dice (rectangular parallelepiped), each having four rectangular faces labelled as $2,3,5$, and $7$. If thrown, the long dice cannot land on the square faces and has $1 / 4$ probability of landing on any of the four rectangular faces. The label on the top face of the dice is the score of the throw.

If thrown together, what is the probability of getting the sum of the two long dice scores greater than $11$?

  1. $3 / 8$
  2. $1 / 8$
  3. $1 / 16$
  4. $3 / 16$

go:426507.title: GATE Mechanical 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 6
go:426507.question:
In the given text, the blanks are numbered $\text{(i)-(iv)}$. Select the best match for all the blanks.

Prof. $\text{P}$   (i)   merely a man who narrated funny stories. (ii) in his blackest moments he was capable of self-deprecating humor.

Prof. $\text{Q}$ (iii) a man who hardly narrated funny stories. (iv) in his blackest moments was he able to find humor.

  1. (i) was (ii) Only (iii) wasn't (iv) Even
  2. (i) wasn't (ii) Even (iii) was (iv) Only
  3. (i) was (ii) Even (iii) wasn't (iv) Only
  4. (i) wasn't (ii) Only (iii) was (iv) Even

go:426509.title: GATE Mechanical 2024 | General Aptitude Question: 8
go:426509.question:
The bar chart gives the batting averages of $\mathrm{VK}$ and $\mathrm{RS}$ for $11$ calendar years from $2012$ to $2022$. Considering that $2015$ and $2019$ are world cup years, which one of the following options is true?

  1. $\mathrm{RS}$ has a higher yearly batting average than that of $\mathrm{VK}$ in every world cup year.
  2. $\mathrm{VK}$ has a higher yearly batting average than that of $\mathrm{RS}$ in every world cup year.
  3. $\mathrm{VK’s}$ yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of $\mathrm{RS}$ between the two world cup years.
  4. $\mathrm{RS’s}$ yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of $\mathrm{VK}$ in the last three years.
